commentary.preText: 10:59 am The Bangladesh players gather for a final huddle before the action begins. Henry Nicholls and Nick Kelly stride out to open the innings for New Zealand, with Taskin Ahmed set to start proceedings with the new ball in hand. Slip and a gully in place. Hashanat Noble: "Everything seems okay right now, though Mustafiz is sidelines at the last moment. I think, Rishad needs to get a break and Tanvir gets chance in the playing eleven of Bangladesh. Rishad is not in a good touch either in PSL." 10:50 am Bangladesh have made a single change to their lineup from their last game, a late adjustment following a final discussion between coach Phil Simmons and skipper Mehidy Hasan Miraz right before the toss. Mustafizur Rahman is sidelined with an injury, knee discomfort, making way for Shoriful Islam. It is a warm and humid day here in Dhaka as the two teams line up for the national anthems: New Zealand followed by Bangladesh. New Zealand XI: 1 Henry Nicholls, 2 Nick Kelly, 3 Will Young, 4 Dean Foxcroft, 5 Tom Latham (capt & wk), 6 Muhammad Abbas, 7 Josh Clarkson, 8 Nathan Smith, 9 Blair Tickner, 10 Will O'Rourke, 11 Jayden Lennox Bangladesh XI: 1 Saif Hassan, 2 Tanzid Hasan, 3 Towhid Hridoy, 4 Najmul Hossain Shanto, 5 Litton Das (wk), 6 Afif Hossain, 7 Mehidy Hasan Miraz (capt), 8 Rishad Hossain, 9 Taskin Ahmed, 10 Nahid Rana, 11 Shoriful Islam Pitch Report from HD Ackerman: "Well, the one thing I will say is that it is extremely warm out here. It's humid, so please excuse me if there's sweat dripping from my brow, but this surface looks a whole lot different from what we've become accustomed to over the years. Normally, it's very dry with loads of grass cuttings thrown over it to hide the dryness, but now we're seeing that a lot of hard work has been done here. This looks as though it's going to have nice pace and good bounce as well, good for the seam bowlers and not a huge amount of turn. It should be nice for batting because the ball will come on to the bat. One thing we need to be aware of is the dimensions of the playing area, because there is a considerably shorter boundary on one side. If you're bowling from the Rocket End, it's just 58 meters to the leg side, whereas the other side of the ground is 69 meters. That is an 11-meter difference. Don't be surprised if, in the powerplay and at the back end of the innings, we see the batters target that short side. When it comes to the toss, I know New Zealand won and said they're going to bat first, but I don't think Bangladesh will mind chasing at all because it looks like a good pitch to bat on." Mehidy Hasan Miraz: "I'd love to bowl first because it's a new ground and a new wicket as well. [Areas to improve] That was a great series for us [Against Pakistan]. The bowlers did really well, especially the pace unit. I think we have a very strong attack at the moment with two spinners and three fast bowlers, so I am really happy. [Pitch] It looks like a good wicket. You know Mirpur; sometimes it favors the batting and sometimes the bowling, so you never really know. Still, I believe this is a very good surface. If we bowl well, we will be in a strong position. [All seamers available?] Yes, we have three seamers." Tom Latham: "We're going to have a bat. It looks like a reasonably good surface, quite dry, and obviously slightly different being a day game. Hopefully, it will potentially slow up towards the back end. [Tour of opportunities?] Absolutely. When guys are playing in different leagues, it presents opportunities to others. We're very fortunate that everyone in our squad has played for New Zealand at some point, but the more exposure and games we can get into these guys to build the depth of New Zealand cricket, the better. It's a really good opportunity for everyone. [Team combination] We've got three genuine seamers, a left-armer, and then a few all-rounders. It's really exciting to get into it; the boys are ready to go. It's been a couple of hot days, but we'll embrace what Bangladesh has for us." New Zealand won the toss and elected to bat first New Zealand Coach Rob Walter: "[Secret recipe to be successful in the subcontinent] No secret formula. We're just trying to play the best cricket we can. Obviously, conditions are the important part of that, so we just try to respond to them as quickly as we possibly can. We pride ourselves on our preparation but also in the way that we adapt quickly; today will be no different. [Number of players making a comeback] I think just having the opportunity is the first thing. There's no downside to experience. Playing against a good quality team in their own conditions puts our younger, inexperienced players under pressure out in the middle, and that's when you find out about your game the best. So, we'll take everything we can get experience-wise and performance-wise, and we'll be ticking boxes the whole way through for sure." Bangladesh Coach Phil Simmons: "It's always good to improve and keep evolving; that's what we've been trying to do. We played well against Pakistan, but now it's a different series. We need to make sure we continue showing that things are getting better from a performance point of view. [Adapting or playing to the conditions?] Well, you've got to play to the conditions. You have to see what the wicket is like and if rain comes in, but you also have to play to the situation happening out there as a player. I think that's what we focus on a lot; controlling the situations on the field." Saleque Sufi : "On a hot humid day at Tigers safe heaven little experienced Black Caps will have challenging encounter against red hot experienced Bangladesh. But in any case New Zealand must not be taken lightly. Expecting a tight and hard fought series. Bangladesh starts favorite but who knows. " ICYMI: Rob Walter sees 54 players from NZ playing in Asia as 'massive positive' 10:20 am As for the visitors, the Black Caps will be missing several big names and regular starters, fielding a relatively inexperienced squad for this white-ball tour. However, seven of these players toured with the 'A' team last year, and two others have prior international experience against Bangladesh, so they aren't entirely green. Injuries have forced some changes, with Ben Lister called up to replace Kristian Clarke. Ben Sears, currently featuring in the PSL, will join the squad for the T20Is. Will O'Rourke, returning to the international stage after an eight-month break, has been vocal about his ambitions for the upcoming tours. It's a massive stage for these players to step up against a full-strength Bangladesh side. For a deeper dive into today's matchups, be sure to check out Mohammad Isam's match preview! 10:10 am Summer hasn't fully arrived in Dhaka yet, but the temperature and humidity are already climbing steadily. Since these day-night fixtures have been converted to day games to save electricity, it's going to be a real physical test for the players out there. Bangladesh have named an unchanged squad from their recent 2-1 series victory over Pakistan. Skipper Mehidy Hasan Miraz has emphasised that they aren't taking New Zealand lightly and is focused on addressing his team's own shortcomings. Rice, fish, and cricket - I think those are the top three priorities for people here in Bangladesh, though the order might vary depending on who you ask! After celebrating the New Year with traditional hilsha and rice, we are back for some sumptuous cricket action. I am Rashad Mahbub, and I'll be joined by Rvel Zahid in the comms later today; we'll be your eyes and ears throughout the match. It's a Friday morning, a weekly holiday in this part of the world, so what are you waiting for? Buckle up and enjoy the ride! 10 am Hello and good morning, ladies and gentlemen from all around the globe. Welcome to ESPNcricinfo's live ball-by-ball coverage of what promises to be a fascinating day of cricket. Just three days into the Bengali New Year, Bangladesh are set to lock horns with New Zealand for a three-match ODI series, followed by a three-match T20I series. We are live from the Shere Bangla National Cricket Stadium in Mirpur for the opening ODI.

commentary.postText: Rifat Emil : "Shariful Islam has been out of ODI cricket for a long time due to various reasons, but his recent form has been impressive overall. Returning to ODIs after 16 months, he has been spot on today, delivering 27 dot balls in his first five overs out of 30 balls."

commentary.postText:
It's time for the first drinks break - a much-needed chance for the players to hydrate in these warm and sunny conditions. New Zealand are cruising along nicely at the moment and will be looking to build a substantial partnership from here.

Orup Ovilashi: "Very surprised to see no Soumya Sarkar in Bangladesh playing XI! Instead they continue with Saif Hasan in opening slot who had a poor run in last ODI series. What could be the reason?"  Chief Selector Habibul Bashar mentioned that the management wants to give the players a few more opportunities, which is why they chose to stick with Saif. They felt the last series was too small a sample size to make a definitive judgment.

commentary.postText: Kamrul: "Bangladesh should seriously consider including a left-arm spinner in the playing XI. Historically, New Zealand have struggled against quality left-arm spin, especially in Asian conditions where pitches assist turn and variation. This could be a key tactical advantage."

commentary.text: Catch it is the cry, but it does not carry to the fielder! Fuller length ball on the pads, plays the conventional sweep shot and connects the leading edge; chips it towards the deep fine leg region. Nahid Rana started his pursuit a bit late and misjudged the trajectory of the ball. He eventually put in a dive but still fell short, with the ball dropping just in front of him.

commentary.text: Massive appeal and given! Nicholls reviews it immediately. Length ball angles in from around the wicket, gets extra bounce off the pitch and thuds the top of the front pad as Nicholls attempts to glance it across the line. Ultra Edge confirms with a spike as the ball passes the bat, a thickish inside edge and onto the pads. The decision is reversed, and Nicholls survives!

commentary.shortText: Nahid Rana to Young, 5 runs
commentary.text: good length ball attacking the stumps, 148 kph ball angling in, defends off the front foot and inside edges onto the pad, scampers across for a single there. Nahid Rana collects the ball and fires a throw at the bowler's end but misses the mark. It's sloppy work from the backup fielder, Tanzid Hasan Tamim, who lets the ball slip right through his legs and trickle away to the long on boundary. Absolutely schoolboy stuff!

commentary.text: A reverse sweep against Rishad, and he's picked up another wicket! Tossed up on a fuller length around the stumps. He gets down on one knee to play the reverse paddle but commits a fraction too early, resulting in a top edge. It's a simple catch for Litton Das behind the stumps, and Nicholls departs after a well-played innings.
commentary.postText: It is time for the second drinks break, and the home team is clawing its way back into the contest. They have sent the dangerous Nicholls back to the pavilion, leaving two fresh batters at the crease to navigate the situation.

commentary.shortText: Nahid Rana to Foxcroft, 1 leg bye
commentary.text: hard length ball on the pads, shuffles across and plays the leg glance stroke, thuds the pad and Nahid appeals for LBW. 148.6 clicks delivery. The umpire is not interested. It is time for Nahid to convince his skipper, who is usually enthusiastic about taking reviews at every opportunity. Miraz sends it upstairs. This one kept a tad low and struck the side of the front pad; to the naked eye, it looks to be heading down the leg. Ultra Edge confirms there is no bat involved. Ball tracking shows it pitched outside off with the impact in line, but it is missing the leg stump. Bangladesh lose their first review.

commentary.text: Struck on the pads and the finger goes up! It's the reverse sweep once again. A length ball on the stumps with a hint of turn; he gets down on one knee to play the reverse paddle but fails to connect, and the ball strikes the top of the front pad. Foxcroft sends it upstairs. It looks like he got lucky there - Ultra Edge shows a thin spike as the ball passes the glove, and he survives!
commentary.preText: Final ten overs to go, and New Zealand will need to shift gears if they want to push past the 260-run mark.

Ultra Edge shows no bat involved, as the ball just brushed the thigh pad. Bangladesh have now lost all of their reviews, and this one was largely on the wicketkeeper, who convinced the skipper to challenge the decision.

commentary.postText: Mohammad Isam informs us that the New Zealand innings took 45 innings more than the scheduled three and a half hours. The reason, according to the press box scorers, is the extra drinks break, apart from an injury break and four DRS calls 3:16 pm It has been a cautious, watchful start for the visitors after winning the toss and opting to bat first on a somewhat slow track where the ball has held up on a few occasions. Just as Kelly was looking to free his arms, he dragged a length ball from Shoriful back onto his leg stump in the 7th over. Young survived an early scare when he was dropped by Saif in the 9th, and he went on to build a steady 73-run partnership with Nicholls before eventually being caught at slip for 30. Latham looked in good touch and found the boundary a few times, but his stay at the crease was cut short when Miraz bowled him. Nicholls also fell victim to the spin, caught behind off Rishad for a well-struck 68 runs. New Zealand's innings was a bit of a mixed bag; they found the right starts and partnerships but struggled to convert them into something truly substantial. Foxcroft benefited from some luck after being dropped by Tanzid on 28 runs at slip, and he made it count by playing a brilliant knock to guide his team toward a competitive total on a pitch with variable bounce. The fielding, however, has been poor today, particularly with the slip catching and ground fielding leaking unnecessary runs. Foxcroft was eventually dismissed for 59 off 58 balls, undone by a clever slower yorker from Nahid Rana. The bowlers continued to pick up wickets, preventing the New Zealand lower order from running away with the game in the death overs. Smith played a useful knock late in the innings to bring his side close to the 250-mark. New Zealand might feel they are 20 to 30 runs short, but credit must go to the bowlers. Shoriful was excellent, picking up two wickets while keeping things tight and economical. Taskin and Rishad also claimed two apiece, while Miraz and Rana chipped in with one each. Rvel will be here to take you through the second innings of the match after a 30-minute break. Bangladesh need 248 runs to win

commentary.text: mean short one fired down the leg-side, cranks up the pace

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Litton Das, no run

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Litton Das, no run

commentary.text: Intimidating short one around the line off, gets on top of the bounce and clobbers it to long-on, one bounce over the rope

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Slows it up, generously flighted outside off, drives it to short cover

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, good length and it kicks back at middle, plays a textbook straight drive but it's so straight that it hits the stumps at non-striker's end

commentary.text: Back of a length with some room to free his arms, runs it down comfortably to deep third

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Pace-off and slightly short meandering outside off, opens the face and whisks it out to point

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Litton Das, FOUR
commentary.text: Drags his length back and the shorter one outside off has been dispatched disdainfully for a four past midwicket! Bangladesh are starting to cut loose now after weathering the opening storm

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Bit short and shapes it back at middle, connected to short midwicket

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: An incredible delivery to punctuate the over! Lands it on a shorter length and gets it to nibble across his blade, nearly edged it, pacers have asked questions every now and then and made the ball talk

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Slip in place. Around the wicket, quicker on a length at middle, punched softly to short cover

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: On a length and turns back at middle, firmly tapped back to the bowler

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Lobbed up on a length or a touch fuller on middle, slinks forward to defend

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: That is a lovely delivery, surprises him with a vicious away turned and he followed it, just past the outside edge

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Floated up fuller and outside off, doesn't spin much on this occasion, blocked to short cover

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, FOUR
commentary.text: Tries to break the shackles and is successful, shimmies down leg and he bolts it fuller on middle, throws his bat at it and a thick outside edge skews away fine of deep third for a four

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, bowled it short and it whizzes in at leg-stump, tries to pull and the ball brushes the glove, ricochets to short fine

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: pitched up on the wickets, blocked hurriedly

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Fraction short and angling into middle and leg, flicks it to deep square

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Slackens the pace and bowls it too full on middle, works it along the carpet to midwicket inside the ring

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: On a length and keeping it tight at middle, shuffles across and gets inside the line to clink it out to short midwicket

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Hammers a shortish ball but it's touch too straight, helps it on its way to long leg
commentary.postText: Hafiz Muhammad : "Too much thing happened in first 10 overs 14 wides , 1 No Ball and 6 on a free hit and back to back wicket as well not sure which side is happy after first 10 overs.''

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Slip in place. Around the wicket, fuller and turning in from wide of off, driven to extra cover

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Bit fuller than previous delivery and tight around the line off, smartly deft touched to cover and steals a run

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Fuller and angled into middle, maneuvered square of midwicket

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, no run
Lennox to Litton Das, 2 runs
commentary.text: Steps away to the leg-side and gives himself room to free arms, clubs it in the air to deep extra cover and it fell away from the man sprinting to his left

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Good length and spinning across off, goes back and slices it out to deep backward point

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Tantalizingly in the air and is lucky he is still out there, Smith sprints from the rope and the ball just lands in front of him, flicked in the air on this shortish ball at leg-stump

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Full and attacks the stumps, blocked off the backfoot

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, FOUR
commentary.text: Overcooked into the toes, puts the rubbish in the bin, just tickles it fine and it runs awax to the rope

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Fuller and slants back into middle and leg, worked wristily to deep square

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Shortish on middle, uppishly smacked a pull to deep square, couple of bounces to the fielder

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Touch full and angled across off, opens the face and sliced it away to deep point

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Around the wicket, full just outside off, driven into the covers, mopped up in the deep

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Flighted fuller on off, beautifully punched to short cover. Neat, compact and organized

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Tossed on middle, covers the stumps and eases it short mid

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Slip in place. Tossed and shaping in at off, lunges forward and pats it to short cover

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Tossed fuller and shaping in at middle, low full-toss, pushed to long-on with a straight bat, playing right under his eyes

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: On a length and sliding across off, reaches out and crouches to carve it away to cover-point inside the 30-yard ring, rollicking along nicely and the partnership is flourishing as New Zealand search for a breakthrough to stay fully alive in the contest

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, bit full and curling back in at off, shuffles a little and powers it to mid-on

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, FOUR
commentary.text: Lands it full on middle, backs away and slugs it out to deep midwicket for a blistering boundary, didn't get the middle but still enough to fetch a four

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, 1 wide
commentary.text: Back of a length and strays down leg, will have to bowl an extra delivery

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, FOUR
commentary.text: Slows down the pace, bowls full on middle without much. movement, lovely wrist-work and plays a picturesque straight drive bisecting long-on and long-off and away she whistles to the boundary

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, FOUR
commentary.text: No slip in place and now the runs are coming thick and fast, slip was very wide, it was around length with width, he went hard at it and a thick outside edge runs away to the rope, seems deliberate

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Good comeback by the bowler, finds that troubling shortish length and gets it to rise steeply, hitting him on the forearm as he tries to negotiate with the awkward bounce

commentary.shortText: Clarkson to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Tad short on middle, covers his stumps and flicks it along the ground to square leg

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Around the wicket, pitched up quick and low on middle, blocked

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Slows it down on this occasion, length and curling it into the pads, lunges and tucks it towards short mid's right

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Length and comes back into middle, connected to square leg

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, 2 runs
commentary.text: Steps across stumps and plays a lovely sweep to deep square, diving stop in the deep saves a couple, batting with fluency now, runs flowing, that's the beauty of ODI cricket; these different passages and how game oscillates from one phase to another

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Loaded full into the toes, makes room and plinks it out to long-off, gallops across for a run

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Again full and fired on middle, opens the face and blocks it to short cover

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, nails the yorker into the blockhole, he digs it out back to the bowler who partially stops it and takes the momentum off, mid-off mops it up further

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Short of a length and hung away outside off, defended to short cover

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Touch short and aiming at middle, deflected square of midwicket inside the ring

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Bit short and rises steeply off the deck on off-stump, jammed down the pitch

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Yanks a shortish ball and floats it with plenty of width, cracked away to short cover

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Bit short and curls back markedly towards off, does well to protect his stumps and fend it out to short cover, well-watched
commentary.postText: JamiL: "Latham doesn't wants to settled BD batters in middle , Five bowlers already been Used inside of 15 overs ."

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Litton Das, no run
commentary.text: Tossed up fuller and outside off, driven along the floor to mid-off

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Litton Das, 1 run
commentary.text: Over the wicket again, touch fuller and outside off, read the line and got inside for a sweep but ends up getting hit on the abdomen and the ball went past the keeper

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: looped up and turns sharply from outside off, lunges forward to defend

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Saif Hassan, 2 runs
commentary.text: Gives himself room and cuts it delectably along the carpet to deep cover-point

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Saif Hassan, 1 run
commentary.text: Propelled fuller just outside off, quickly gets behind the line and pushes it with a straight bat to long-on

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Litton Das, FOUR
commentary.text: Once again too full and getting predictable with this ploy, width was there, he charges and insolently smashed into the gap at mid-off for a four, high hands and lovely use of the feet to generate power, beautiful swing!

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, fraction short and curving back at off, defended with remarkable solidity and grit to short cover, comfortable out there

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, no run
commentary.text: Bustles in and lands on a length at middle, prodded to short cover

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Saif Hassan, OUT
commentary.text: Oh-ohww! That's exactly what the doctor prescribed for New Zealand to spice things up in this contest! O'Rourke, the deck-hitting enforcer, hits the pitch hard and gets prodigious bounce, he takes him on tries to whack a pull but it hits him high on the bat and he ends up giving a regulation catch to mid-on, end of a terrific knock!

dismissal.text: Saif Hassan c Clarkson b O'Rourke 57 (76b 8x4 1x6) SR: 75

commentary.text: On a length and angled in sharply towards off, gets in line and works it to midwicket spaces. Tidy and a successful over!

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Tight lines! Good length outside off on a lower trajectory, bunted out to short cover er

commentary.shortText: Foxcroft to Litton Das, OUT
commentary.text: Flight and drift, comes back extravagantly from outside off, kicking from a length and beats him all ends up, that was unplayable! What a delivery, hits the top of middle and the zingers are flying in all directions! Needed an outstanding delivery to get the better the technician and this was indeed a superb one!

commentary.text: Hitting the deck, goes with the natural angle to off, punched back tot he bowler, riveting contest continues, Bangladesh need another partnership

commentary.text: gosh! that is another spectaclar delivery, what a bowler! Good length and spins away markedly, bouncing steeply as well, somehow he survives!

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Towhid Hridoy, no run

commentary.text: Fuller and shaping into his shin, worked around the corner

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Touch fuller and sprayed wide of off, clatters it to short cover
commentary.postText: Marco: "It feels like Bangladesh are just doing enough - at some stage they might find they need to do a bit more, and that will be interesting. When will they accelerate"

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Around the wicket, fuller and wide of off, sumptuously driven but straight to the fielder at short cover
commentary.postText: Atanu Sen : "The pitch is not straight forward for batting you need to spend some time before playing big shots and also the percentage of dot balls in middle overs needs to be reduced because this has been a problem for Bangladesh Cricket Team over the last few years strike rotation is also important the pitch will be under the radar once again for uneven bounce I think Bangladesh should win this it will be a real shame if Bangladesh doesn't win today."

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Hits the deck hard, midriff height on middle, lobbed up and lands short of the fielder at short midwicket
commentary.postText: DaWolf: "This pair is clearly just taking it carefully and trying not to lose their wicket, problem is that the run rate is going up and if it gets to ~8 they will be vulnerable to a collapse. They need to be scoring just that little bit quicker (4-5RPO not less than 3)."

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, 2 runs
commentary.text: Back of a length and holding its line wide of off, steered along the carpet to deep third
commentary.postText: Mahi: "How more days will Bangladeshi players take to learn to make innings bigger? We, fans, are just tired of seeing them througing wickets after being set. This is very much painful to watch in the age of modern day cricket where centuries are made often."

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Fraction short and whizzes back from wide of off, kept low as he tried to hack it away, dot
commentary.postText: DaWolf: "Bangladesh can afford to just play out Lennox who only has 2 overs left, but they will need to be a touch more aggressive against the other bowlers. RRR already past 6 now. "

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, pitched up outside off, stabs it away to sweeper cover

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Around the wicket, good length and just inside the tramline on off, attempted a square cut and not able to execute it properly, hits near the toe-end and dies quickly

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Slowish shortish delivery floating outside off, crisply cut away to deep third for a single

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Back of a length and hung away wide of off, tapped away to deep cover-point

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Around, shortish and shapes it back on middle, swiped back to bowler playing it through the line, terrific bowling

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Back of a length and curled back at off-stump, deft touch towards third man up in the ring, acrobatic stop to keep them down to one

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, 1 wide
commentary.text: Around the wicket, short and outside leg-stump, some exercise for the umpire

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Whew! Good length and came back sharply towards middle, eased to short cover

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Shortish and brings it back on middle, sliced towards backward point, not letting him get off the hook

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, 2 runs
commentary.text: Pulls his length back and bowls a touch straight, lifts his front leg and smacks it uppishly to deep backward square, fumble allows them to pick up a couple, Smith got an awkward bounce and ran in too hard from the rope

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, 2 runs
commentary.text: Good length and came back sharply at middle, beautiful clean swing of the bat and clears mid-on but the holds up near the rope, nevertheless a lovely high-handed full swing of the bat

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Pitched shortish on off, opens the face and glide sit down to deep third, nice rotation of the striker, asking rate is not much big of a problem but they got to prevent a collapse and keep knocking the ball around, wait for looseness to pounce on

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, around length and angled across off, square cuts it with soft hadst and sets off for a quick single

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, 2 runs
commentary.text: Around the wicket, deck hit hard around the line off, driven to wide of mid-off, fielder hits the stumps and non striker's end, ball deflects away and they get another run, made his ground well before the ball shattered the stumps, not out on big screen

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Touch short and came back towards off-stump, inside-edge deflects away to deep fine

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: No boundary since 24th over. Over the wicket, fuller and attacking middle, comes on front foot and whisks it away to deep midwicket

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Back of a length just outside off, beautiful jab to deep cover-point

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Towhid Hridoy, FOUR
commentary.text: Over the wicket, pitched short and wide, asking for trouble and gets trouble! Slammed away into the gap at deep backward point to end the boundary drought!

commentary.shortText: O'Rourke to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Attempted yorker ends up as a low full-toss and he almost handed a catch to short midwicket while walking down the wicket

commentary.shortText: Smith to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Around the wicket, came wide of the crease and belted into the deck on a shortish length, gets that nip back into off, easily steered down to deep third, smart cricket, taking the game deep, not throwing caution to the wind

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Charging in with spring in his step, over the wicket and lands it short on off, kicked back, defended to the bowler, has been the pick of the bowlers for NZ

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Sharp shorter in-slanter towards middle, covers the line and forces it to deep midwicket

commentary.shortText: Smith to Afif Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Around the wicket, shorter and a touch wide, slammed a pull to mid-on

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Liquid-rolling smooth run-up, length on an irksome off-stump line, shuffles and taps it back to Smith

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: gotta love the discipline of NZ quicks, rarely bowled a poor delivery. Hits a length on this occasion and gets it to swerve back at off unless it's an optical illusion, driven firmly to mid-on

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Towhid Hridoy, 2 runs
commentary.text: Around the wicket, full and outside off, sits up nicely for him to play a late cut into the gap at deep third and that's a useful boundary, need more of these, at-least one every two overs

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Bowls full into the toes, plays an attacking sweep to deep fine

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Afif Hossain, OUT
commentary.text: 50 partnership came up. Over the wicket, floated generously and fuller outside off, he takes the bait, skedaddles down the track and attempts to whack it over long-on but hasn't got all of it and has been snaffled by O'Rourke, crucial and a timely breakthrough that once again tilts the balance of the game slightly in the favor of New Zealand or the jury is out, I mean it's such a tight game, anybody's game. Bangladesh would need to stay calm and composed, take calculated risks.

dismissal.text: Afif Hossain c O'Rourke b Lennox 27 (49b 0x4 0x6) SR: 55.1

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Lobbed up on a length from around the wicket and it turns away appreciably, beating the outside edge, ripped and turned and the bowler surprised himself

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Tries again, gets even more turn on this occasion, intentionally angled it further in and got it to grip and turn and bounce sharply! beating him again, he is loving bowling on this surface

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Tossed up fuller on middle, covers his stumps and is content to block it

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Rushed him into the stroke, appeal for an lbw turned down. Over the wicket and short of a length on middle, gets on front foot to flick it away and misses it, height could be an issue, but maybe worth taking another look, seemed pretty adjacent

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, 1 wide
commentary.text: Banged it short on middle, tries a swivel pull and guides it behind square, but he has bowled too short and let's see how costly this error proves to be

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Not that costly, bowls a serving short one around the line off, rides the bounce and awkwardly fended to short third

commentary.shortText: Smith to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, 1 run
commentary.text: Touch short and shapes it back towards middle, gets some inward seam movement, worked bristly to square leg, diving stop prevents any thoughts of a pair of runs

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Hits the deck hard and slants it on middle, attacks it and hauls it away to deep square, only a single

commentary.shortText: Smith to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, 1 run
commentary.text: Over the wicket, short of a length around the line off, lovely steer off the backfoot with an open face to deep third
commentary.postText: Prosanjit: "Bangladesh missing the trick again, Afif and Towhid were dragging it deep. Towhid needs to go big now and close the gap, or this gets tight… and we know how that usually ends."

commentary.shortText: Smith to Towhid Hridoy, no run
commentary.text: Good length with a hint of swing outside off, slapped away to short cover, diving stop
commentary.postText: MD Shaminul Hos: "I wonder why Bangladesh does not have a powerhitter in ODIs? India has KL Rahul and Hardik Pandya, England has Jos Buttler and Will Jacks, South Africa has Dewald Brevis and Marco Jansen. Bangladesh has none. And the pitches are not a reason, as these teams play well in tough conditions as well."

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, 2 runs
commentary.text: Around the wicket, fuller and outside off, big yawning aerial sweep towards deep backward square, good intent to score and keep the asking rate in check

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Fired down full on middle, square drive to cover

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Flighted full and wide of off, almost a full-toss, blocked to the bowler

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Pushed fuller at off-stump, spins away and almost kissed the off-stump, marvelous delivery!

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: Oh my goodness, this is awesome, pulls his length back and gets huge turn across off, misses the off again

commentary.shortText: Lennox to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, 1 run
commentary.text: Angled towards middle and he knocks it to mid-on for a single, what a spell, 1-32 and used the conditions brilliantly, deserved more wickets

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, no run
commentary.text: over the wicket, bit shot on middle and leg, clipped to square leg up in the ring. Tension is palpable, crowd completely hooked

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, 1 run
commentary.text: Back of a length and angled in at middle, swivel pull to deep fine leg

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Towhid Hridoy, 1 run
commentary.text: Climbs on him from a short length and offers width, rides the bounce and upper-cuts to deep third

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Mehidy Hasan Miraz, OUT
commentary.text: good length and slanted across off, tries a cheeky little inverse reverse lap and ends up guiding it to Latham who takes a brilliant diving catch to his right, big breakthrough!

dismissal.text: Mehidy Hasan Miraz c †Latham b Tickner 6 (14b 0x4 0x6) SR: 42.85

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Rishad Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Pitched fuller on middle and off with a hint of wobble in the air, feebly poked into the covers

commentary.shortText: Tickner to Rishad Hossain, 1 run
commentary.text: Dug in short and width on offer, hacked square of midwicket for a run

commentary.shortText: Smith to Rishad Hossain, no run
commentary.text: Touch short and comes back in a shade from fifth stump line on off, shuffle across and tentatively pokes it to short cover, too circumspect for my liking
